Cookie Policy

Last updated: 18 April 2026 | Version 1.0 | Governed by UK PECR & UK GDPR

Your choices matter. Essential cookies keep the site working. All other cookies are optional and only activate with your consent.

1. What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. This policy covers all tracking technologies including local storage, session storage, and pixel tags.

  • First-party cookies — set by slp-media.com
  • Third-party cookies — set by external services (Meta, analytics)
  • Session cookies — deleted when you close your browser
  • Persistent cookies — remain for a set period

2. UK Law and Cookies

Cookie use in the UK is governed by PECR 2003, interpreted with UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. We must obtain your prior, informed, freely given consent before placing non-essential cookies, and give you a genuine way to refuse or withdraw.

3. Cookie Categories

Essential Cookies Always active

Required for the site to function. Cannot be disabled.

CookieSet byPurposeDuration
slp_cookie_consentSLP MediaStores your consent preferences12 months

Analytics Cookies Requires consent

Help us understand how visitors use our site. Data is anonymised. Only active with your consent.

CookieSet byPurposeDuration
_gaGoogle AnalyticsDistinguishes users2 years
_ga_XXXXXXXXGoogle AnalyticsSession state2 years

Marketing & Advertising Cookies Requires consent

Used for targeted advertising via Meta/Facebook. Only active with your consent.

CookieSet byPurposeDuration
_fbpMeta PixelAd delivery & conversion tracking3 months
_fbcMetaClick attribution2 years
frMetaAd retargeting3 months

4. Third-Party Services

ServicePurposeMore info
CalendlyMay set cookies on their booking interfacePrivacy Policy
Meta PixelConversion tracking (consent only)Privacy Policy

5. Managing Your Preferences

5.1 Our preference centre

5.2 Browser settings

  • Google Chrome
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Apple Safari
  • Microsoft Edge

5.3 Opt out of advertising

  • Meta: Facebook Ad Settings
  • Google: adssettings.google.com

6. Withdrawing Consent

Withdraw consent any time via our preference centre. Takes immediate effect for new cookies. Delete existing cookies via your browser settings. Withdrawal does not affect prior lawful processing.

7. Updates to This Policy

We review this policy regularly. The "Last updated" date reflects the latest revision.
